nodes.label: SCFF (paper story)
nodes.rationale: Nature Communications 2025: fixes Forward-Forward's broken negative-source by pairing (positive=self-pair, negative=cross-pair) instead of label-stamped fakes; goodness=mean squared activation (energy). Adopted as the cheap unsupervised 80% brain of draft 6.0.

nodes.label: Forward-Forward's broken negative source
nodes.rationale: Hinton 2022 FF needed a wrong-label-stamped fake as the negative, which needs labels and only works on MNIST-easy tasks; the weak link SCFF fixes.

nodes.label: Self-contrastive pairing (positive=self, negative=other)
nodes.rationale: Positive = sample paired with itself; negative = paired with a different sample; contrast comes from data structure alone, no labels touched — turns FF into a clean unsupervised learner.

nodes.label: Goodness = energy (G = mean squared activation)
nodes.rationale: Each layer's goodness is the mean of squared activations; push positive-energy above threshold, negative-energy below — later shown (Phase 3) to be the objective that fails to compose with depth.

nodes.label: Sum reformulation (vs paper's concatenation)
nodes.rationale: The paper concatenates [x_k,x_k]/[x_k,x_n] with two weight blocks that provably converge equal (W1=W2); draft-6.0 sums instead (one weight, half the input lines) — algebraically identical, and is what makes mono-forward (one vector, one shared crossbar) possible.

nodes.label: SCFF = the cheap brain / unsupervised 80%
nodes.rationale: SCFF is the only surveyed learning rule that is local, derivative-free, forward-only, and unsupervised at once — that combination is why it can live on the analog substrate; matches backprop on easy tasks, gap opens as tasks get harder.

nodes.label: SCFF's hard-task gap is the reason the GD 20% exists
nodes.rationale: SCFF organizes the world cheaply but can't coordinate across layers toward a label; the GD block is bolted on to do exactly the cross-layer coordination SCFF gives up — defines the 80/20 split's rationale.
